data.title: Superior Group of Companies Declares Regular Quarterly Cash Dividend
data.description:
ST. PETERSBURG, Fla., Feb. 06, 2025 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) — The Board of Directors of Superior Group of Companies, Inc. today announced that it has declared a quarterly dividend of $0.14 per share, payable February 28, 2025, to shareholders of record as of February 18, 2025.

About Superior Group of Companies, Inc. (SGC):

Established in 1920, Superior Group of Companies is comprised of three attractive business segments each serving large, fragmented and growing addressable markets. Across Healthcare Apparel, Branded Products and Contact Centers, each segment enables businesses to create extraordinary brand engagement experiences for their customers and employees. SGC’s commitment to service, quality, advanced technology, and omnichannel commerce provides unparalleled competitive advantages. We are committed to enhancing shareholder value by continuing to pursue a combination of organic growth and strategic acquisitions. For more information visit www.superiorgroupofcompanies.com.

Contact:

Investor Relations

Investors@superiorgroupofcompanies.com

data.title: Superior Group of Companies Announces Participants for Year 2 of Its Board Observer Program
data.description:
– Designed to Expose Traditionally Underrepresented Groups to Public Company Corporate Governance –

– SGC Doubles the Size of its Board Observer Program –

– Ixchell Duarte and Tauana McDonald Named as Participants –

ST. PETERSBURG, Fla., Feb. 06, 2025 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) — Superior Group of Companies, Inc. , is pleased to announce the launch of the second year of its Board Observer Program and a doubling of the number of participants.

In February 2024, SGC launched a program designed to provide governance exposure to those traditionally underrepresented in public company boardrooms. Participants in the non-voting Observer Program are invited and expected to attend most of our Board and committee meetings over the course of a year and are encouraged to ask questions as well as lend their own perspective to those meetings. The goal is to provide participants with a firsthand understanding of the responsibilities and dynamics involved in serving on a public company board. This exposure is expected to contribute to their professional development and enhance their knowledge of corporate governance practices in order to help them be better positioned to join a public company board. There is no expectation that the program participants will join SGC’s board.

“SGC’s Observer Program was an overwhelming success in its first year, with Dr. Kelly Richmond Pope setting a high bar for future participants”, said Michael Benstock, Chairman of the Board of Directors and Chief Executive Officer of SGC. “As intended, the program provided a diverse perspective to our already diverse Board, but, more importantly, provided invaluable hands-on, real world experience to a person that has a bright future. We are ecstatic about the individuals selected to participate in the second year of the program, and look forward to continuing the program indefinitely.”

“As stated a year ago upon the program’s launch, it is – and remains – our hope that this program will be an example that other company boards can use to help level the playing field,” said Todd Siegel, Chair of SGC’s Corporate Governance, Nominating & Ethics Committee. “The first year of the program established proof of concept, and we look forward to continuing and expanding the program’s value with two superb participants in year two.”

The program’s observers this year are Ixchell Duarte and Tauana McDonald.

For more information about Ms. Duarte, please click here.

For more information about Ms. McDonald, please click here.

About Superior Group of Companies, Inc. (SGC):

Established in 1920, Superior Group of Companies is comprised of three attractive business segments each serving large, fragmented and growing addressable markets. Across Healthcare Apparel, Branded Products and Contact Centers, each segment enables businesses to create extraordinary brand engagement experiences for their customers and employees. SGC’s commitment to service, quality, advanced technology, and omnichannel commerce provides unparalleled competitive advantages. We are committed to enhancing shareholder value by continuing to pursue a combination of organic growth and strategic acquisitions. For more information visit www.superiorgroupofcompanies.com.

Contact:

Investor Relations

investors@superiorgroupofcompanies.com

Attachments

  • Duarte
  • McDonald

data.title: Conoco Quarterly Net Income Fell, Expects 2025 Production Growth — Commodities Roundup
data.description:
MARKET MOVEMENTS:

  • Brent crude oil is flat at $74.63 a barrel
  • European benchmark gas is up 1.5% to 53.23 euros a megawatt-hour
  • Gold futures are down 0.5% at $2,877.90 a troy ounce
  • LME three-month copper futures are flat at $9,271.50 a metric ton

TOP STORY:

Conoco Quarterly Net Income Fell, Expects 2025 Production Growth

Conoco Phillips said fourth-quarter net income declined alongside energy prices but the oil giant forecast production growth in 2025 while signaling a more aggressive dividend and share-buyback campaign following the recent closure of its multibillion-dollar Marathon Oil acquisition.

The Houston oil giant posted earnings of $2.31 billion, or $1.90 a share, down from $3 billion, or $2.52 a share, a year earlier. On an adjusted basis, excluding the impact of one-off items tied to the Marathon Oil purchase, ConocoPhillips earned $1.98 a share, eclipsing the average Wall Street expectation of $1.83 a share, as tallied by FactSet.

Conoco said cash provided by operating activities amounted to $20.1 billion.

OTHER STORIES:

Hershey Earnings Beat Forecasts, but High Cocoa Prices Sour Outlook

Hershey's quarterly profit and sales beat expectations, but the candy maker warned that surging cocoa prices will hurt its financial performance this year.

Chief Executive Michele Buck said cocoa prices would "put significant pressure" on earnings. On an adjusted basis, the Pennsylvania-based chocolatier forecasts earnings per share will fall in the mid-30% range for 2025.

--

Anglo American Warns of De Beers Impairment Amid Subdued Diamond Demand

Anglo American expects its De Beers diamond business to book an impairment as the mining giant reviews the value of the unit it hopes to offload.

The London-listed miner said Thursday that the review would examine De Beers's value in light of continued subdued demand for diamonds as it seeks to exit the business. Last year, Anglo wrote down the book value of De Beers by $1.6 billion to $7.6 billion.

--

Anglo American Cuts 2025 Diamond View After Production Falls

Anglo American reported a fall in fourth-quarter copper and diamond production and cut its 2025 diamond production guidance.

The London-listed mining giant said Thursday that it dug out 198,000 tons of copper, 14% less than in the fourth quarter of 2023, due to the planned shut down of its smaller Los Bronces plant.

--

ArcelorMittal Banks on Improved Steel Demand After Booking Narrowed Net Loss — Update

ArcelorMittal said it believes demand for steel will improve this year after fewer impairment charges and other exceptional items led to a narrowed net loss in the last quarter of 2024.

The Luxembourg-based steelmaker said that it expects apparent steel consumption, a measure of steel demand, to rise by 2.5% to 3.5% this year compared with 2024 when excluding China. ArcelorMittal expects this increase to support growth in steel shipments.

--

Orsted Expects Earnings to Rise This Year as It Works to Shore Up Balance Sheet

Orsted guided for slightly higher earnings this year, a day after it slashed spending plans for the coming few years to shore up its balance sheet.

The Danish renewable-energy company announced late Wednesday that it will cut its investment program by 25% in the period to 2030 as it switches its focus to only the most financially attractive projects, ensuring a stronger balance sheet and supporting a solid investment grade rating.

--

Power Stocks Had Been on an AI Tear. Then Came DeepSeek.

Chinese AI startup DeepSeek has laid bare one of the power sector's greatest fears: The AI boom might not need nearly as much electricity as anticipated.

DeepSeek, a generative-AI model trained using substantially less energy than its American competitors, routed tech and power company shares last week, eroding stock gains spurred by data center growth projections. Now, the companies planning to power those data centers are grappling with the prospect that AI may become much more energy efficient, and fast.

--

Adnoc Gas's Profit Beat Driven by Renegotiated Emirates Utility Contract

Adnoc Gas, one of the world's biggest natural-gas producers, reported a market beating fourth-quarter net income as it benefited from a renegotiated contract with the Emirates Water and Electricity Company.

The Abu Dhabi-owned gas company on Thursday said net profit for the quarter rose to $1.38 billion from $1.35 billion for the same period a year earlier, beating a $1.03 billion Visible Alpha consensus.

--

BP Europa Seeks Buyer for German Refinery ROG

BP Europa SE said it intends sell its German business Ruhr Oel GmbH-BP Gelsenkirchen, without disclosing financial details.

The oil-and-gas company on Thursday said the process to find a suitable acquirer will start immediately, with the sales agreement expected for 2025.

--

Edison Unit Says Its Equipment May Have Been Involved in SoCal Fires

A subsidiary of Edison International said there may be a link between its equipment and two of the wildfires that raged in Southern California last month.

Southern California Edison on Thursday submitted two letters to the California Public Utilities Commission with updates on its analysis of the Eaton and Hurst wildfires, saying it believes its equipment may be associated with the start of the Hurst fire.

--

Matthews Intl Says Arbitrator Rules in Its Favor in Tesla Lawsuit

Matthews International said an arbitrator ruled in its favor in a lawsuit between the company and electric vehicle maker Tesla.

The electric vehicle battery supplier said the arbitrator acknowledged its background that predates Tesla and that it has the right to sell its solutions to customers other than the electric vehicle maker.

--

Aurubis Shares Jump on Higher Revenue, Earnings

Aurubis shares rose on Thursday after the German metals supplier posted higher revenue and adjusted earnings, following a rise in metal prices.

In midday trading, shares traded 4.9% higher at 78.45 euros.

--

Gold Fields Sees Gold Production in Line With Target

Gold Fields said it expects a drop in gold production for 2024, but in line with its target range.

The South African gold miner on Thursday said it forecasts full-year gold-equivalent production of 2.07 million, down from 2.30 million in 2022.

MARKET TALKS:

U.S. Natural Gas Inventory Draw Matches 5-Year Average

1052 ET - U.S. natural gas inventories declined by 174 billion cubic feet last week, in line with the five-year average for the period, the EIA reports. At 2,397 Bcf, gas in underground storage at the end of January was 111 Bcf below the five-year average and 208 Bcf below the year-ago level. Analysts in a Wall Street Journal survey had forecast a 170 Bcf drawdown for last week. Nymex natural gas is off 1.2% at $3.319/mmBtu, supported on the downside by some colder weather foreseen for mid-February. (anthony.harrup@wsj.com)

--

Hershey Says Sweets Category Is Eating Into Chocolate's Share — Market Talk

1009 ET - Hershey said its volume of chocolate sales decreased in its most recent quarter, which CEO Michele Buck attributed in part to convenience stores facing pressure. Consumers also continue to be focused on health and wellness, she said, with the chocolatier seeing growth in zero-sugar and protein products. Successful innovation in the sweets category has also contributed, Buck said, highlighting Shaq-a-licious sour gummies and freeze dried Jolly Ranchers as two recent examples, and will come to Reese's Peanut Butter Cups later this year. (roshan.fernandez@wsj.com)

--

Oil Futures Recover Ground in Choppy Trade — Market Talk

0950 ET - Oil futures are recouping some of the previous day's losses, while the market continues to ponder the impact of trade tariffs, rising U.S. crude stocks, and prospects of a step-up in OPEC+ output in April. "Most traders feel the U.S. tariff threats to China are for real and could damage their economy further, and with OPEC set to raise production soon, WTI prices may have heavier headwinds in the future," BOK Financial's Dennis Kissler says in a note. "Look for a choppy type trade until more is known about tariffs." WTI is up 0.7% at $71.53 a barrel, and Brent is 0.6% higher at $75.02.(anthony.harrup@wsj.com)

--

Hershey CEO Says High Cocoa Prices Driving Demand for Alternatives — Market Talk

0949 ET - Cocoa alternatives are in growing demand given high prices for the real thing, Hershey CEO Michele Buck said. Some companies feeling the pressure have switched to cocoa butter alternatives, she said, reducing demand in the global cocoa market. "We do that where possible, but we're pretty precious about the brands and what they stand for with consumers, " Buck said. (roshan.fernandez@wsj.com)

--

ArcelorMittal Plans Electrical Steel Production in Alabama — Market Talk

0947 ET - Steelmaker ArcelorMittal says it intends to build a plant in Alabama to produce the paper-thin steel used in the motors of electric vehicles, air conditioners and other products. Construction of the $1.2-billion plant at ArcelorMittal's steel mill near Mobile, Ala., will start later this year. Production is projected to begin in 2027. The plant will be able to produce about 165,000 tons a year. Production of electric vehicles is expected to drive higher demand for electrical steel, which accounts for about 1% of the worldwide steel production annually with most of it now in Asia. The only U.S. producers of electrical steel are Cleveland-Cliffs and U.S. Steel, which recently began production in Arkansas. (robert.tita@wsj.com )

--

U.S. Natural Gas Futures Await Inventory Data 0948 ET - U.S. natural gas futures are lower ahead of the EIA's weekly storage report, but holding ground with some colder weather returning to mid-February forecasts. Natural gas storage likely fell by 170 Bcf to 2,401 Bcf in the last week of January, according to a Wall Street Journal survey, leaving stocks at a 107 Bcf deficit to the five-year average. The Nymex front month is down 1.3% at $3.317/mmBtu. (anthony.harrup@wsj.com)

--

Arabica Coffee Sets New Record on Supply Concerns — Market Talk

1407 GMT - Arabica coffee futures rise to a record high on supply worries. ICE coffee futures rose 1.7% to around $4.05 a pound in European afternoon trading. They have climbed as high as $4.11 earlier in the session. The recent price rises reflect persistent supply concerns, especially in Brazil, ING analysts say in a note. There are suggestions that market conditions will remain tight as exchange inventories continue to fall, while the next harvest is set to suffer from bad weather, ING says. Adverse weather prior to October deteriorated harvest prospects for the crop, and market participants have reported bleak forecasts. Coffee futures have notched successive highs in nearly every session since mid-January. (joseph.hoppe@wsj.com)

--

Gold Futures Tick Lower But Hold Close to Highs on Safe-Haven Demand — Market Talk

1358 GMT - Gold futures fall, but keep close to record highs on macroeconomic and geopolitical uncertainty. Futures are down 0.4% at $2,881.10 a troy ounce, after hitting a fresh record of $2,906 an ounce in the prior session. The precious metal is keeping near highs amid trade war concerns, ING analysts say in a note. The risk of economic conflict is risking higher inflation and slower growth, spurring demand for safe-haven assets, ING says. While trade and tariff uncertainty continue to boost precious metal prices, U.S. President Trump's latest comments that the U.S. should take over the Gaza strip and assume responsibility for reconstructing the territory have added to gold's bullish momentum, ING says. (joseph.hoppe@wsj.com)

--

Anglo American's Expected De Beers Impairment Shows Diamonds Aren't Forever — Market Talk

1147 GMT - Anglo American's preparations to write down the value of its De Beers unit shows why it is so keen to jettison the business, says AJ Bell's investment director Russ Mould. The London-listed miner cut 2025 diamond production guidance as it looks to stabilize supply while clearing its large inventory, he says. De Beers cut prices by 10% to 15% in its last auction of 2024 as demand faltered, he says. It remains to be seen whether Anglo American is vulnerable to a bid from a rival after it fought off interest from BHP last year, he adds. Shares trade up 6.5% at 2483.5 pence. (adam.whittaker@wsj.com)

--

Base Metals Rise on Weaker Dollar, Return of Chinese Buyers — Market Talk

0938 GMT - LME three-month copper is up 0.6% at $9,325 a metric ton and LME three-month aluminum is up 0.4% at $2,633.50 a ton. Base metals prices have gained on a moderate risk-on sentiment, on a weaker U.S. dollar and the gradual return of Chinese participants to the market after the Lunar New Year holidays, Sucden Financial's Daria Efanova says. A continued increase in liquidity will further support the metals complex over the coming days, Efanova says in a note. Copper is firmly back above key resistance levels and is up 2.75% on week. Aluminum remains relatively subdued, up just 0.55% on week. That said, aluminum's spot price is higher than the main benchmark futures contract, indicating the market is fundamentally tight, Efanova adds. (joseph.hoppe@wsj.com)

--

Petronas Faces Limited Impact From Change to Sarawak Gas Sector — Market Talk

0931 GMT - Petronas may face a lower financial impact than initially expected following a recent policy shift in Sarawak's gas sector, Citi analyst Steven Chan says in a note. Sarawak's state-owned Petroleum Sarawak will now manage only domestic gas supply, while LNG exports remain under Petronas's purview. Concerns had risen that Petronas's revenue and free cash flow could be affected as Petroleum Sarawak became the sole gas aggregator in 2H 2024. Chan notes that LNG exports account for 94% of Sarawak's natural gas output and 27% of Petronas's 2023 revenue. He views the development as positive, easing concerns over potential upstream capital expenditure cuts by Petronas and ensuring the Malaysian state-owned energy company retains exclusive control over exploration and distribution with no disruptions to existing agreements. (yingxian.wong@wsj.com)

--

Europe's Gas Price Rises on Colder Weather Forecast

0931 GMT - European natural-gas prices rise in early trade on expectations of stronger demand due to a cold snap. "Forecasts for colder weather have raised concerns that we will see steeper draws in gas storage in the coming days," ING strategists Warren Patterson and Ewa Manthey say. EU inventories have been depleting faster than usual, with storage levels at 51.3% and daily net withdrawals of around 7.9 terawatt-hours as of Tuesday. The benchmark front-month contract at the Dutch TTF hub currently trades 1.3% higher to 54.13 euros a megawatt-hour and is up almost 15% on the month. Prices rebounded in the previous trading session after China said it would impose tariffs on U.S. LNG starting on Feb. 10, a move expected to free up larger volumes for other buyers, including Europe. (giulia.petroni@wsj.com)

--

Gold Futures Slip but Keep Near Record; Bull Market to Persist, Citi Says — Market Talk

0919 GMT - Gold futures tick lower, but keep near record highs. Futures slide 0.4% to $2,881.30 a troy ounce, after hitting a fresh record of $2,906 in the prior session. Gold's bull market looks set to continue under the Trump administration, with trade wars and geopolitical tensions reinforcing trends toward central bank reserve diversification and de-dollarization, Citi analysts say. Support from emerging market central bank gold demand and global growth concerns should raise both ETF and physical gold demand, they say in a note. A Russia-Ukraine peace deal, and clarity around whether gold will be exempt from blanket U.S. tariffs could provide a buying opportunity over the coming months, the U.S. bank writes. Citi raises its 0-3 month price target for London gold to $3,000 an ounce, from $2,800, and its 2025 average price to $2,900 from $2,800 an ounce. (joseph.hoppe@wsj.com)

--

Oil Edges Higher After Saudi Arabia Hikes Selling Prices — Market Talk

0905 GMT - Crude futures find some support in early trade after Saudi Arabia lifted prices for March loadings of all crude grades it exports amid surging premiums for Middle Eastern oil. State-owned oil giant Aramco's flagship Arab Light crude to Asia, its main market, was raised by $2.40 a barrel to $3.90 a barrel over the Oman/Dubai benchmark--the largest monthly increase since August 2022. "This ties in with the strength that we have seen in the Middle East physical market since the start of the year," ING analysts say. Brent crude is up 0.1% at $74.71 a barrel, while WTI rises 0.3% to $71.22 a barrel. The benchmarks settled 2% lower in the previous trading session due to a larger-than-expected build in U.S. crude stockpiles and fears that a trade war between the U.S. and China could hurt global growth. (giulia.petroni@wsj.com)

--

Gold May Rise Further Near-Term on Tariff Risks, Robust Central Bank Demand — Market Talk

0726 GMT - Gold may rise higher than previously forecast in the near term owing to tariff risks and robust central bank demand, Citi Research's Kenny Hu says. The precious metal has rallied 10% year-to-date, breaching Citi's up to three-month target of $2,800/oz,the analyst says in a research report. Citi raises this target to $3,000/oz while keeping its 6-12 month target of $3,000/oz unchanged. Gold's bull market appears set to persist with trade wars and geopolitical tensions reinforcing the reserve diversification and de-dollarization trend and supporting emerging-market central banks' demand, the analyst adds. Spot gold is 0.2% lower at $2,861.07/oz. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

--

Brent Crude Oil May Hover Between $60-$65 a Barrel Through 2H — Market Talk

0512 GMT - The scenario for Brent crude oil at $60-$65 per barrel through 2H just got stronger, Citi Research's Francesco Martoccia says in a research report. An increase in non-OPEC+ oil supply remains poised to outpace weak global demand growth from 2Q through 4Q, with the oil market shifting into surplus, the energy strategist says. "We remain strongly of the view that President Trump could ultimately prove to be a bearish influence on the oil market," Martoccia says. He notes that Trump has consistently highlighted lower energy prices as the central solution to issues such as U.S. inflation. Front-month Brent crude oil futures are 0.1% higher at $74.69/bbl; front-month WTI crude oil futures are 0.2% higher at $71.18/bbl. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

--

China's EV Sector Unlikely to See Aggressive Price Cuts This Year — Market Talk

0506 GMT - The magnitude of price cuts in China's electric-vehicle industry this year should be much less than last year, Jefferies analysts write in a note. Since inventories at BYD dealers are much lower and the profitability of new car sales is much better, the analysts reckon BYD is unlikely to kick off an aggressive price war like last year. Among the top Chinese EV makers, Xiaomi, Huawei-backed Harmony Intelligent Mobility Alliance and XPeng are outperformers by new order intakes, they note. The introduction of Tesla's full-self driving features in China this year could serve as a good example for its Chinese rivals on how carmakers can commercialize their advanced assisted driving technologies, they add. (jiahui.huang@wsj.com; @ivy_jiahuihuang)

--

Toyota's Shanghai EV Factory Plan Is a Step Toward Electrification Target — Market Talk 0405 GMT - Toyota Motor's plan to build a new Lexus factory in Shanghai to develop and produce electric vehicles and batteries is a positive for China's auto sector, Daiwa analyst Kelvin Lau writes in a note. The newly formed entity in Shanghai will develop an EV model under the Lexus brand, with production to start from 2027. The initial production capacity will be around 100,000 units annually. The plan reflects Toyota's commitment to the Chinese EV market, and implies opportunities for the carmaker's existing and potential partners, Lau says. Daiwa remains cautiously positive due to limited details regarding the Shanghai factory, but it's an important step for Toyota to achieve its 2035 electrification goal. (jiahui.huang@wsj.com; @ivy_jiahuihuang)

--

Most Iron Ore Futures Edge Lower Amid Lingering Tariff Worries — Market Talk

0153 GMT - Most iron ore futures edge lower in the morning Asian session amid lingering tariff worries. China this week said it will impose new tariffs on select U.S. imports, of which coal will be subject to a 15% tariff. Both iron ore and coal are used in steel production. "Concerns of weaker export-driven demand amid rising trade tensions are likely to weigh on sentiment in the near term," ANZ Research analysts say in a research report. The most-traded May futures are 0.3% lower at CNY803.5/ton on the Dalian Commodity Exchange. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

Write to Barcelona Editors at barcelonaeditors@dowjones.com

data.title: JPMorgan Adjusts Hologic Price Target to $85 From $94
data.description: Hologic has an average rating of Hold and mean price target of $82.53, according to analysts polled by FactSet.

data.title: Mizuho Securities Adjusts Hologic Price Target to $83 From $87
data.description: Hologic has an average rating of Hold and mean price target of $82.53, according to analysts polled by FactSet.

data.title: Carvana's New ABS Deal Eases Investor Concerns on Loan Sales, Wedbush Says
data.description:
Carvana's new non-prime asset-backed securities deal has reassured investors but the smaller deal size and limited loan seasoning indicate that the company sold non-prime loans through other channels rather than delaying sales to Q1 2025, Wedbush said in a note on Thursday.

The firm said that while non-prime loans sold outside asset-backed securities channels generally have lower margins, the downside risk to Carvana's Q4 other gross profit per unit, or GPU, is minimal because consensus estimates have already adjusted downward, and stronger-than-expected retail GPU should offset any weakness.

Wedbush said that compared with Q3 and Q4, "Q1 2025 should have a seasonally higher mix of non-prime loan sales that have higher margins than prime loan sales, so pressure from compression in spreads could be muted by favorable mix."

The firm said that the new deal features slightly better loan quality, including higher borrower credit scores and lower interest rates, while the overall portfolio remains mixed and that lower interest rates combined with higher benchmark rates may lead to lower excess spreads.

Wedbush has a neutral rating on Carvana's stock with a price target of $250.

data.title: AgriFORCE Growing Systems Optimizes Sturgeon, Alberta Facility with New BITMAIN Antiminer S19kPro Miners Amid Favorable Market Dynamics
data.description:
Vancouver, British Columbia, Feb. 06, 2025 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) — AgriFORCE Growing Systems Ltd. , a pioneering technology-driven innovator in sustainable digital asset mining and agricultural solutions, today announced the successful acquisition and near term deployment of 220 new BITMAIN Antiminer S19kPro miners at its Sturgeon, Alberta mining facility. This strategic move, replacing the 134 existing miners, is set to nearly triple the facility’s hashrate, dramatically enhancing operational capacity and profitability.

Strategic Operational Enhancements in Alberta

Operating in Alberta offers AgriFORCE a significant competitive edge. The province’s naturally colder climate minimizes cooling challenges and reduces energy expenses—eliminating the need for costly immersion cooling strategies and ensuring optimal uptime without significant heat or dispatch issues. Coupled with Alberta’s robust oil and gas expertise—often referred to as the “Texas of the North”—the region provides unparalleled opportunity for operational stability and efficiency.

Competitive Market Advantages

AgriFORCE is favorably positioned in the market due to several key factors:

  • Favorable U.S. Tariffs: Recent U.S. tariffs of 25% on foreign-manufactured mining equipment have further reinforced the competitive advantage of AgriFORCE’s Alberta operations, where procurement benefits from an in excess of 40% discount in foreign currency arbitrage compared to USD purchases. With revenue earned in USD per BTC, these conditions significantly boost the economic efficiency of the facility.
  • Forward Negotiations with the Trump Administration: We believe that ongoing forward negotiations with Alberta’s representatives and the new Trump administration have created an environment of regulatory certainty and supportive policies, paving the way for future expansion and innovation in the region.

Operational Impact and Future Growth

With the new acquisition, AgriFORCE’s Sturgeon facility is on track to triple its hashrate, positioning the Company to capitalize on increasing digital asset demand while simultaneously benefiting from the cost efficiencies and reliability of operations in a resource-rich, cold climate. Barrett Mooney, COO of AgriFORCE, stated:

“Our growth plan centers on the optimization of our existing assets. The integration of 220 new BITMAIN Antminer S19kPro miners at Sturgeon is a prime example of how we maximize operational efficiency and profitability. Alberta’s naturally cold climate, combined with our strategic financial advantages and a supportive regulatory environment, positions us well ahead of our competitors.”

Jolie Kahn, CEO of AgriFORCE, commented:

“We have a unique business model that targets immediate revenue generation through assets that are ready to produce from day one. By leveraging the operational benefits of Alberta—where lower cooling costs and superior oil and gas expertise create an environment akin to the ‘Texas of the North’—we are able to sidestep grid dependency and avoid operational curtailments during power price surges. Additionally, the recent U.S. tariffs and our advantageous currency arbitrage further enhance our profitability. Our proactive negotiations in the province and with the new Trump Administration’s policies only serve to strengthen our strategic position and fuel our aggressive growth trajectory.”

About AgriFORCE Growing Systems Ltd.

AgriFORCE Growing Systems Ltd. is a forward-thinking technology company dedicated to solving critical challenges in digital asset mining, sustainable agriculture, and environmental stewardship. By leveraging advanced technologies, innovative business models, and strategic market positioning, AgriFORCE is committed to delivering long-term value for shareholders while creating lasting benefits for communities and ecosystems. For more information, visit www.agriforcegs.com.

Forward-Looking Statements

This press release contains forward-looking statements that involve risks and uncertainties, including statements regarding anticipated operational performance and future growth. Actual results may differ materially from those projected. Factors that could cause actual results to differ include, among others, market volatility, regulatory changes, and operational risks detailed in AgriFORCE’s filings with the Securities and Exchange Commission. AgriFORCE assumes no obligation to update or revise these forward-looking statements.

For Media Inquiries, Please Contact:

Richard Wong

CFO, AgriFORCE Growing Systems Ltd.

Phone: 1-778-658-0533

Email: rwong@agriforcegs.com

Follow AgriFORCE on Social Media:

  • X: @agriforcegs
  • Facebook: AgriFORCE Growing Systems Ltd.
  • LinkedIn: AgriFORCE Growing Systems Ltd.

data.title: Roblox Stock Sinks on Bookings Miss and Weak Forecast — Barrons.com
data.description:
By Mackenzie Tatananni

Roblox stock plunged in premarket trading Thursday after the online gaming company missed analysts' targets for fourth-quarter bookings and daily active users.

Shares fell 21% to $59.51 as Roblox posted bookings of $1.36 billion in the quarter. While the figure represented a 21% increase from the previous year, it fell short of analysts' calls for $1.37 billion.

The company also reported 83.5 million daily active users, up 19% from the prior year, but sharply missing analysts' expectations of 88.39 million.

CEO David Baszucki said the company would continue to invest in its virtual economy, app performance, and "AI-powered discovery and safety" in 2025.

Roblox said it anticipates full-year bookings in the range of $5.2 billion to $5.3 billion. The midpoint of the range was below analysts' estimates of $5.3 billion, according to FactSet.

Write to Mackenzie Tatananni at mackenzie.tatananni@barrons.com

This content was created by Barron's, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. Barron's is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: Market Chatter: Trump to Meet With US Steel CEO Thursday

US Steel did not immediately reply to a request for comment from MT Newswires.

(Market Chatter news is derived from conversations with market professionals globally. This information is believed to be from reliable sources but may include rumor and speculation. Accuracy is not guaranteed.)

data.title: Volcon Closes $12 Million Unit Offering; Shares Fall
data.description:
Volcon said Thursday it closed a public offering of 6 million common units at $2 apiece for gross proceeds of about $12 million.

Each unit consists of one common share or pre-funded warrant and one registered common warrant to purchase one share.

The company expects to use the net proceeds, along with its existing cash, for general corporate purposes and working capital.

Volcon shares were down 6.9% in recent trading.

data.title: Lavras Gold Closes $15 Million Public Offering
data.description:
Lavras Gold (LGC.V on Thursday said it completed a best-efforts public share offering, raising $15 million including the full exercise of the over-allotment option.

The company issued 6.8-million shares at $2.20 each in the financing.

Proceeds will be used for exploration and development of the Lavras do Sul Project in southern Brazil.

Lavras Gold was last seen unchanged at $2.12 on the TSX Venture Exchange.

data.title: BASF Unit Shifts Two US Sites to Renewable Energy
data.description:
BASF said Thursday two US sites of its coatings division are now using full renewable electricity as of January.

The changes at the facilities in Greenville, Ohio, and Blackman Township, Michigan, support the German chemicals company unit's sustainability goals.

data.title: CAC 40 Hits 8-Month High, Lifted by Strong Earnings
data.description:
The CAC 40 rose 1.5% to close at 8,008 on Thursday, reaching an eight-month high, driven by robust corporate earnings across several stocks.

Societe Generale led the index, surging 13.2% to its highest level since February 2022, after the bank reported a doubling of Q4 profits and announced shareholder payouts.

ArcelorMittal followed suit, climbing 13.3% to its highest level since April 2023, supported by Q4 sales of $14.71 billion, up from $14.55 billion a year earlier.

Pernod Ricard also outperformed, gaining 3.2% after posting stronger-than-expected earnings.

On the domestic political front, the 2025 budget bill is set for final approval in the Senate on Thursday, following a no-confidence motion against Prime Minister Bayou, which garnered only 128 votes in the 577-seat National Assembly.

data.title: These Stocks Are Moving the Most Today: Skyworks, Qualcomm, Lilly, Ford, Honeywell, Roblox, Peloton, Tapestry, Amazon, and More — Barrons.com
data.description:
By Joe Woelfel

Stocks rose modestly Thursday as investors looked past worries over tariffs and turned their attention to falling bond yields and corporate earnings.

These stocks were moving Thursday:

Qualcomm posted fiscal first-quarter adjusted earnings of $2.75 a share, beating Wall Street estimates of $2.46, as revenue jumped 17% to $11.67 billion. Revenue at the company's QCT semiconductor business rose 20% in the period to $10.1 billion. But intellectual property licensing revenue of $1.54 billion missed estimates of $1.56 billion. Qualcomm said it expects fiscal second-quarter revenue of $2.38 to $2.58 a share on revenue of $10.3 billion to $11.2 billion. Shares of the chip maker were falling 5.6%.

Chip designer Arm Holdings reported fiscal third-quarter adjusted earnings that beat analysts' estimates but its guidance for its fourth quarter matched forecasts, disappointing investors and sending the stock down 7%. For the fourth quarter, Arm said it expects adjusted earnings of 52 cents a share on sales of $1.23 billion at the midpoint of its guidance. Wall Street anticipates fourth-quarter earnings of 52 cents a share on sales of $1.22 billion.

Skyworks Solutions plunged 26% after the wireless chip maker said it expected its share of the business to supply Apple, its biggest customer, with radio frequency components would decline by 20% to 25%. The drop, Skyworks said in a conference call, would begin impacting revenue in its fiscal fourth quarter and throughout fiscal 2026. The company also named Philip Brace, who most recently served as CEO of 5G mobile company Inseego, as its next president and CEO.

Honeywell International announced Thursday a plan to break into three companies in an effort to unlock shareholder value. The industrial conglomerate also reported solid fourth-quarter earnings but issued profit and revenue guidance for 2025 that was below analysts' estimates. Shares fell 4.3%.

Ford Motor fell 6.5% after the auto maker posted fourth-quarter operating profit of $2.1 billion on sales of $48.2 billion, better than analysts' estimates on both metrics, but its forecast for operating profit this year of between $7 billion and $8.5 billion missed expectations of about $9.3 billion. Ford also said it expects breakeven adjusted earnings before interest and taxes in the first quarter because of "lower wholesales and unfavorable mix," and expenses related to the launch of some major U.S. assembly plants.

Eli Lilly, the drugmaker, posted fourth-quarter earnings that beat Wall Street expectations. Adjusted earnings in the period were $5.32 a share, better than estimates of $5.01. The company said it sees 2025 adjusted earnings in the range of $22.50 to $24 a share. The midpoint of the guidance tops analysts' expectations of $22.76. The stock was up 0.9%.

Bristol Myers Squibb declined 2.4% after fourth-quarter earnings soundly beat Wall Street consensus but management's guidance for the 2025 was short of expectations.

Roblox sank 15% after the online-gaming company posted fourth-quarter bookings and average daily active users that rose from a year earlier but missed Wall Street forecasts.

Peloton Interactive jumped 21% after the maker of at-home fitness equipment reported better-than-expected fiscal second-quarter revenue and boosted fiscal-year guidance for adjusted earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation, and amortization to a range of $300 million to $350 million.

Tapestry was up 16%. The owner of Coach, Kate Spade, and Stuart Weitzman reported fiscal second-quarter earnings that beat Wall Street estimates and raised its fiscal-year outlook.

MicroStrategy, the largest corporate holder of Bitcoin, fell 1.4%. The company, which announced Wednesday it now was doing business as Strategy, posted a fourth-quarter loss of $670.8 million, or $3.03 a share. Revenue fell 3% to $120.7 million and missed analysts' estimates of $122.4 million. The company also said it saw its largest-ever increase in quarterly Bitcoin holdings in the period, "culminating in the acquisition of 218,887 bitcoins acquired for $20.5 billion" since the third quarter. The company also said it has so far bought $20 billion of Bitcoin under a three-year plan of buying $42 billion in the cryptocurrency.

Molina Healthcare fell 7.4% after fourth-quarter adjusted earnings of $5.05 a share missed analysts' expectations of $5.88 and the healthcare-services provider said it expected 2025 adjusted earnings of at least $24.50 a share, below analysts' estimates of $25.82.

FormFactor, the provider of test and measurement technologies, was falling 11% after issuing a weak first-quarter forecast. The company said it expects adjusted earnings of 15 cents to 23 cents a share on revenue of $165 million to $175 million. Analysts had been expecting adjusted earnings of 33 cents a share on revenue of $192.9 million.

Meta Platforms rose for a 13th-straight session on Wednesday, the stock's longest winning streak on record. Shares of the parent of Facebook and Instagram also set an intraday high of $718.14, and closed at a record high of $704.87. Meta was up 0.3% Thursday at $706.90.

Earnings reports are expected after the closing bell Thursday from Amazon.com, Fortinet, Cloudflare, and Take-Two Interactive Software.

Amazon.com was up 0.8%. Analysts expect the tech and online retailing giant to report fourth-quarter earnings of $1.49 a share on revenue of $187.3 billion. Wall Street will be watching Amazon's web services business closely after rival tech companies' cloud segments posted disappointing results. Amazon's report is scheduled for after the closing bell Thursday.

Write to Joe Woelfel at joseph.woelfel@barrons.com

This content was created by Barron's, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. Barron's is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: US Natgas Prices Edge Up after EIA Data
data.description:
US natural gas futures rose above $3.3/MMBtu, supported by a larger-than-expected storage draw.

The EIA reported a 174 billion cubic feet (bcf) withdrawal from storage for the week ending January 31, slightly above analysts’ expectations of 168 bcf.

This draw was also larger than last year’s 110 bcf decline and matched the five-year average.

Additional support came from higher flows to LNG export plants, which averaged 14.9 billion cubic feet per day (bcfd) in early February, up from 14.6 bcfd in January and surpassing the December 2023 record of 14.7 bcfd.

Looking ahead, colder-than-normal conditions from February 10-14 are expected to increase heating demand.

data.title: Senegal's 2025 oil output at Sangomar to reach 30.5 mln barrels, ministry says
data.description:
Crude oil output from Senegal's Sangomar oil and gas field is expected to reach 30.53 million barrels this year, exceeding its 2024 production of 16.9 million, the West African country's energy ministry said on Thursday.

Discovered 10 years ago about 100 km (62 miles) south of the capital Dakar, Sangomar is expected to produce about 100,000 barrels per day (bpd).

Sangomar's output in January reached 3.11 million barrels of crude oil, the ministry said in a statement published on X, adding that 3 cargoes, representing a total volume of 2.89 million barrels, were shipped and sold on the international market.
